revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 尾張戸神社 |画像 = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[瀬戸市]]十軒町845
愛知県[[名古屋市]][[守山区]]大字志段味字東谷209 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=尾張戸神社}} |祭神 = [[天火明命]]
[[天香語山命]]
[[建稲種命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = （伝）第13代[[成務天皇]]5年 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 熱田の奥の院・東谷明神・当国明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]][[神社本庁]]調査部編『神社名鑑』（神社本庁、1962年）p. 447。 |神事 = }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|尾張戸神社の鎮座する[[東谷山]]}}]] '''尾張戸神社'''（おわりべじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]・[[瀬戸市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[東谷山]]（とうごくさん）の[[山頂]]に鎮座する。別称として「熱田の奥の院」や「東谷明神」・「当国明神」とも{{R|由緒書|明治}}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱祭神の記載は神社由緒書による。『日本の神々』では、3柱のほか[[乎止与命]]・天道日女命を合祀するとする。。いずれも[[尾張氏]]の遠祖とされる神々である。 * '''[[天火明命]]'''（あめのほのあかりのみこと） *: 尾張氏祖神。 * '''[[天香語山命]]'''（あめのかぐやまのみこと） *: 別名を「[[高倉下|高倉下命]]（たかくらじのみこと）」。天火明命の長子。 * '''[[建稲種命]]'''（たけいなだねのみこと） *: 天火明命十二世孫。 祭神のうち天香語山命（高倉下命）は、[[庄内川]]対岸の高蔵山に降り立ち、のちにこの東谷山に移ったという{{R|由緒書}}。この際に白鹿に乗って川を渡ったといい、その地に架かる「[[鹿乗橋]]」に伝承の名残を残している{{R|由緒書|神々}}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝（「東谷大明神草創本紀」）によると、第13代[[成務天皇]]5年に[[宮簀媛|宮簀媛命]]の勧請によって創建されたという神社由緒書。{{R|toki74}}。宮簀媛命は[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の妃で、建稲種命の妹にあたる。 また、江戸時代末期から明治時代初期にかけて刊行された地誌『[[尾張名所図会]]』では、尾張戸神社が鎮座する「東谷山」の由来について、「尾張戸の神を鎮まります山」なので古くは「尾張山」と呼ばれていたが、尾張の国名を表す「当国（當国、とうごく）」から「当国山（當国山、とうごくやま）」と称されるようになり、のちこれが転じて「東谷山」と書き表す様になったと伝える。 東谷山西麓には多数の[[古墳]]があり、尾張戸神社本殿も[[円墳]]「尾張戸神社古墳」の墳丘上に鎮座する。「尾張戸」の社名を「おわりと」と呼ばずにの「おわりべ」とするのは「尾張部」を意味するともいわれ{{R|地名}}、この場合尾張氏に従属した[[部民]]を意味する。これらの古墳は尾張氏の墓であると伝えられる{{R|由緒書}}。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には[[尾張国]][[山田郡 (尾張国)|山田郡]]に「尾張戸神社」と[[式内社]]の記載がある『愛知県の地名』尾張戸神社項。。また平安時代末期の『[[尾張国内神名帳]]』では「尾張戸天神」と記載があり{{R|地名}}、現在ではこれらは当社に比定されている{{R|地名}}。かつては[[熱田神宮]]に次ぐ大社であったとする伝えもある志段味古墳群をときあかす、P.74。 [[中世]]には、[[大永]]元年（[[1521年]]）7月17日に起きた火災によって[[神宮寺]]と共に焼失、これに伴い神宮寺は[[廃寺]]となった上志段味誌、P.93。同年12月には[[守護]]の[[斯波氏]]により社殿が再興されたが、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には荒廃した上志段味誌、P.114。 [[江戸時代]]に入り、初代[[尾張藩|尾張藩主]]の[[徳川義直]]の時、当地から「当国明神」銘の鉄筒が出土した{{R|地名}}。当時の当社は「東谷大明神」と称していたが、この時に「東谷」は「当国」すなわち「尾張国」からの変化であると見なされ、当社は『延喜式』神名帳所載の「尾張戸神社」にあてられるとともに、祭神は尾張氏祖神となされたという{{R|地名|神々}}。その後の『尾張名所図会』では、前述のように由緒の説明がなされている。 [[名古屋城]]の[[鬼門]]の位置に鎮座することから[[尾張徳川家]]の信仰は篤く、2代藩主の[[徳川光友]]により[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）には中社・南社・薬師堂の造営、寛文11年（[[1671年]]）には社殿の修造が行われ{{R|kamishidami114|地名}}『明治神社誌料』尾張戸神社項。、それ以後も藩費による営繕が行われた{{R|由緒書}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）5月に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した。 === 神階 === * [[従二位]]、[[従三位]]、または[[正四位]]下 （『[[尾張国内神名帳]]』） - 表記は「尾張戸天神」。位階は写本により異同がある。 ** 従二位：[{{NDLDC|991014/415}} 『神社覈録 上編』]（皇典研究所、1902年、[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）415コマ。 ** 従三位：『明治神社誌料』尾張戸神社項。 ** 従三位上：神社由緒書、『愛知県の地名』尾張戸神社項。ただし「従三位上」という[[位階]]はない。 ** 正四位下：{{PDFlink|[http://www.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 『国内神名帳』]}}（文政13年（1830年）転写合本、愛知県図書館「貴重和本デジタルライブラリー」より）12コマ。 == 境内 == 本殿はかつて[[八幡造]]であったが、[[明治時代]]に[[神明造]]に建て替えられた上志段味誌、P.115。 また、尾張戸神社の北西側をやや下った場所に小堂がある。これは[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）の参道整備の際に見つかった中世の[[甕棺墓]]を現地に埋め戻して祀ったもので、「甕室明神」と呼ばれている 本文編、P.31本文編、P.44。 == 摂末社 == * 中社（なかやしろ/なかのやしろ、{{ウィキ座標|35|15|18.11|N|137|3|8.99|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|位置|name=境内社：中社}}） *: 祭神：[[菊理媛|菊理媛命]]{{R|神々}}。円墳「中社古墳」（[[#中社古墳|後述]]）の墳丘上に鎮座する。[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に二代藩主・[[徳川光友]]が薬師堂とともに創建『日本の神々』尾張戸神社項。。 * 南社（みなみやしろ / みなみのやしろ、{{ウィキ座標|35|15|12.80|N|137|3|8.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|位置|name=境内社：南社}}） *: 祭神：[[イザナギ|伊弉諾命]]{{R|神々}}。円墳「南社古墳」（[[#南社古墳|後述]]）の墳丘上に鎮座する。中社同様、寛文5年（1665年）の創建{{R|神々}}。 File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|中社 File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|南社 == 古墳 == === 尾張戸神社古墳 === {{日本の古墳 |画像 = [[File:Owaribe-jinja honden.JPG|250px]]
墳丘全景（墳頂に尾張戸神社本殿） |名称 = 尾張戸神社古墳 |所属 = [[志段味古墳群]] |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]] |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 墳径27.5[[メートル|m]] |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]前半 |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「尾張戸神社古墳」（「[[志段味古墳群]]」に包含） |出土品 = 山茶碗片など |}} '''尾張戸神社古墳'''（おわりべじんじゃこふん）は、尾張戸神社の本殿下にある[[円墳]]。[[志段味古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「志段味古墳群」のうち）{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1458|志段味古墳群}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf 「史跡志段味古墳群保存管理計画」]}}（名古屋市ホームページ）。。 [[埴輪]]を伴わない点・[[葺石]]の類似点から、山麓にある[[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥塚古墳]]（志段味古墳群の代表古墳）と同時期の[[4世紀]]前半の築造とされる志段味古墳群をときあかす、P.6。 ==== 規模・構造 ==== この尾張戸神社古墳の形式について、かつては[[前方後方墳]]・[[前方後円墳]]との説があった。[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）あるいはその前年に[[京都大学|京都帝国大学]]の[[梅原末治]]によって概略図が作成されており{{R|honbun31}}、当時は円墳と想定されていた。 [[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）6月に[[名古屋市教育委員会]]文化財保護室によって初めての試掘調査が行われ中日新聞：[http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/aichi/20080621/CK2008062102000041.html 古代の豪族「尾張氏」の墓か　東谷山山頂の古墳３基]、2008年6月21日 {{リンク切れ|date=2013年3月}}、2009年の本調査で[[円墳]]とされた。この調査によって墳丘は直径約27.5[[メートル]]で2段築成と推測されているが、2段目は神社の社殿造営のためにほとんどが削平されている{{R|honbun44}}。 1段目は墳裾から高さ約2メートル、傾斜は30-35度で本文編、P.35、葺石は角礫主体{{R|honbun44}}。基底石の大きさは約30[[センチメートル]]で、上部は10-20センチメートルの礫が使用されており{{R|honbun35}}、[[石英]]の小礫が混ざっていた{{R|honbun44}}。葺石を配置したのちに蒔かれた可能性もあるとされる。1段目のテラス部は幅1.2-1.4メートルで、石英の小礫が葺かれていた{{R|honbun35}}。 2段目は30センチメートルほどの基底石とその上側の一部のみ、残存が確認されている。葺石は1段目と同様に10から20センチメートルの小礫が使用されていた。[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）に本殿裏側が掘り返されて盗掘されかけるも巨石に阻まれた事で被害を免れた記録があるが、この巨石は[[竪穴式石室]]の天井石と考えられている{{R|honbun31}}。周溝は確認されていない{{R|honbun44}}。 [[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）10月6日、古墳域は[[白鳥塚古墳]]（[[1952年]]に既指定）・中社古墳・南社古墳などとともに「[[志段味古墳群]]」として国の史跡に指定された{{R|志段味古墳群}}。 ==== 出土品 ==== 古墳からの主な出土品は次の通り。 * [[山茶碗]]片 * 四耳壺片（[[古瀬戸]]） * [[土師器]]皿片 * [[瓦]] * 敷瓦 * [[皇宋通宝]] * [[寛永通宝]] * 桐1銭青銅貨（[[大正]]8年・大正9年のもの） 梅原が調査した際の聞き取りで「埴輪が見つかったことがある」との記録があるが、実物は確認されていない。また、近年の調査においても築造時期を明確に示す遺物の出土はない。山茶碗や四耳壺片、土師器皿などは中世遺物で、瓦類は近世のものであった本文編、P.41 - 44。なお、[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）の参道整備の際に見つかった[[甕棺墓]]は15-16世紀の[[常滑焼]]の大甕で、中から針状鉄製品や人骨片、歯牙、木炭片などが見つかっている{{R|honbun31}}。また、かつて墳丘の北東側で[[渥美窯]]で焼かれたと考えられる[[経筒]]容器の破片が見つかった記録があるという{{R|honbun44}}。 === 中社古墳 === [[File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro-kofun.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|中社古墳}}]] '''中社古墳'''（なかやしろこふん）は、尾張戸神社の境内社・中社の社殿下にある[[前方後円墳]]。志段味古墳群を構成する古墳の1つで、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「志段味古墳群」のうち）{{R|国指定|志段味古墳群}}。 東谷山山頂から南約120メートルの地にある、墳丘長約63.5メートルの前方後円墳である境内説明板。。斜面には川原石の葺石があり、円筒埴輪が巡らされている{{R|説明板}}。これらの墳形・埴輪から、尾張戸神社古墳に続く4世紀中頃の築造とされる{{R|説明板}}。 === 南社古墳 === [[File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro-kofun.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|南社古墳}}]] '''南社古墳'''（みなみやしろこふん）は、尾張戸神社の境内社・南社の社殿下にある円墳。志段味古墳群を構成する古墳の1つで、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「志段味古墳群」のうち）{{R|国指定|志段味古墳群}}。 東谷山山頂から南の鞍部を越えた位置にある、直径約30メートルの円墳である{{R|説明板}}。かつては前方後円墳とする説もあった{{R|説明板}}。墳丘の上段には円礫、下段には角礫が葺かれる珍しい形式を有する{{R|説明板}}。出土した円筒埴輪が中社古墳と同形式であることから、本古墳も同じく4世紀中頃の築造とされる{{R|説明板}}。 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === * [[愛知県]][[瀬戸市]]十軒町845 * 愛知県[[名古屋市]][[守山区]]上志段味東谷2099 === 交通アクセス === * [[東海旅客鉄道]]（JR東海）[[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]] [[高蔵寺駅]]から、登山口まで徒歩で約15分、山頂まで徒歩で約20分。 * [[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]] [[中水野駅]]から、登山口まで徒歩で約20分、山頂まで徒歩で約15分。 * [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]：高蔵寺行き）で「東谷橋」バス停下車、登山口まで徒歩で約10分、山頂まで徒歩で約20分。 * 東谷山散策路（[[東谷山フルーツパーク]]正門横から）で、山頂まで徒歩で約20分。 === 周辺 === * [[志段味古墳群]] - 上志段味に展開する古墳群。尾張戸神社との関係が指摘される[http://www.museum.city.nagoya.jp/exhibition/special/past/tenji120428.html 企画展「尾張氏☆志段味古墳群をときあかす」]（名古屋市博物館）。。 ** [[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥塚古墳]] - 志段味古墳群の代表古墳。愛知県内第3位の大きさの前方後円墳。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書、境内説明板（名古屋市教育委員会設置） * 明治神社誌料編纂所編『[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]』（明治神社誌料編纂所、1912年）尾張戸神社項 ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/794}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）794-795コマ参照。 * 『日本歴史地名大系 愛知県の地名』（[[平凡社]]、1981年）瀬戸市 尾張戸神社項 * 名古屋市上志段味特定土地区画整理組合 『上志段味誌』、1997年 * [[名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]編 『埋蔵文化財調査報告書62 志段味古墳群（本文編）』名古屋市教育委員会、2011年 * [[名古屋市博物館]] 『尾張氏・志段味古墳群をときあかす』、2012年 * 津田豊彦「尾張戸神社」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海』（[[白水社]]、1987年）） * [[尾張名所図会|『尾張名所図会』 第三巻　尾張戸神社]]、1844年 == 関連項目 == * [[尾張氏]] * [[熱田神宮]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|n|1670604707}} * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html 名古屋市:史跡　尾張戸神社古墳（おわりべじんじゃこふん）] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=尾張戸神社古墳 |1-1=愛知県の古墳 |1-2=守山区の歴史 |1-3=愛知県にある国指定の史跡 |1-4=円墳 |redirect2=中社古墳 |2-1=愛知県の古墳 |2-2=守山区の歴史 |2-3=愛知県にある国指定の史跡 |2-4=前方後円墳 |redirect3=南社古墳 |3-1=愛知県の古墳 |3-2=守山区の歴史 |3-3=愛知県にある国指定の史跡 |3-4=円墳 }} {{デフォルトソート:おわりへしんしや}} [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:守山区の建築物]] [[Category:瀬戸市の歴史]] [[Category:瀬戸市の建築物]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おわりへしんしや]]

旧[[郷社]] |創建 = （伝）第13代[[成務天皇]]5年 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 熱田の奥の院・東谷明神・当国明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]][[神社本庁]]調査部編『神社名鑑』（神社本庁、1962年）p. 447。 |神事 = }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|尾張戸神社の鎮座する[[東谷山]]}}]] '''尾張戸神社'''（おわりべじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]および[[瀬戸市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 [[東谷山]]（とうごくさん）の[[山頂]]に鎮座する。別称として「熱田の奥の院」や「東谷明神」・「当国明神」とも{{R|由緒書|明治}}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱祭神の記載は神社由緒書による。『日本の神々』では、3柱のほか[[乎止与命]]・天道日女命を合祀するとする。。いずれも[[尾張氏]]の遠祖とされる神々である。 * '''[[天火明命]]'''（あめのほのあかりのみこと） *: 尾張氏祖神。 * '''[[天香語山命]]'''（あめのかぐやまのみこと） *: 別名を「[[高倉下|高倉下命]]（たかくらじのみこと）」。天火明命の長子。 * '''[[建稲種命]]'''（たけいなだねのみこと） *: 天火明命十二世孫。 祭神のうち天香語山命（高倉下命）は、[[庄内川]]対岸の高蔵山に降り立ち、のちにこの東谷山に移ったという{{R|由緒書}}。この際に白鹿に乗って川を渡ったといい、その地に架かる「[[鹿乗橋]]」に伝承の名残を残している{{R|由緒書|神々}}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝（「東谷大明神草創本紀」）によると、第13代[[成務天皇]]5年に[[宮簀媛|宮簀媛命]]の勧請によって創建されたという神社由緒書。{{R|toki74}}。宮簀媛命は[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の妃で、建稲種命の妹にあたる。 また、江戸時代末期から明治時代初期にかけて刊行された地誌『[[尾張名所図会]]』では、尾張戸神社が鎮座する「東谷山」の由来について、「尾張戸の神を鎮まります山」なので古くは「尾張山」と呼ばれていたが、尾張の国名を表す「当国（當国、とうごく）」から「当国山（當国山、とうごくやま）」と称されるようになり、のちこれが転じて「東谷山」と書き表す様になったと伝える。 東谷山西麓には多数の[[古墳]]があり、尾張戸神社本殿も[[円墳]]「尾張戸神社古墳」の墳丘上に鎮座する。「尾張戸」の社名を「おわりと」と呼ばずにの「おわりべ」とするのは「尾張部」を意味するともいわれ{{R|地名}}、この場合尾張氏に従属した[[部民]]を意味する。これらの古墳は尾張氏の墓であると伝えられる{{R|由緒書}}。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には[[尾張国]][[山田郡 (尾張国)|山田郡]]に「尾張戸神社」と[[式内社]]の記載がある『愛知県の地名』尾張戸神社項。。また平安時代末期の『[[尾張国内神名帳]]』では「尾張戸天神」と記載があり{{R|地名}}、現在ではこれらは当社に比定されている{{R|地名}}。かつては[[熱田神宮]]に次ぐ大社であったとする伝えもある志段味古墳群をときあかす、P.74。 [[中世]]には、[[大永]]元年（[[1521年]]）7月17日に起きた火災によって[[神宮寺]]と共に焼失、これに伴い神宮寺は[[廃寺]]となった上志段味誌、P.93。同年12月には[[守護]]の[[斯波氏]]により社殿が再興されたが、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には荒廃した上志段味誌、P.114。 [[江戸時代]]に入り、初代[[尾張藩|尾張藩主]]の[[徳川義直]]の時、当地から「当国明神」銘の鉄筒が出土した{{R|地名}}。当時の当社は「東谷大明神」と称していたが、この時に「東谷」は「当国」すなわち「尾張国」からの変化であると見なされ、当社は『延喜式』神名帳所載の「尾張戸神社」にあてられるとともに、祭神は尾張氏祖神となされたという{{R|地名|神々}}。その後の『尾張名所図会』では、前述のように由緒の説明がなされている。 [[名古屋城]]の[[鬼門]]の位置に鎮座することから[[尾張徳川家]]の信仰は篤く、2代藩主の[[徳川光友]]により[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）には中社・南社・薬師堂の造営、寛文11年（[[1671年]]）には社殿の修造が行われ{{R|kamishidami114|地名}}『明治神社誌料』尾張戸神社項。、それ以後も藩費による営繕が行われた{{R|由緒書}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）5月に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した。 === 神階 === * [[従二位]]、[[従三位]]、または[[正四位]]下 （『[[尾張国内神名帳]]』） - 表記は「尾張戸天神」。位階は写本により異同がある。 ** 従二位：[{{NDLDC|991014/415}} 『神社覈録 上編』]（皇典研究所、1902年、[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）415コマ。 ** 従三位：『明治神社誌料』尾張戸神社項。 ** 従三位上：神社由緒書、『愛知県の地名』尾張戸神社項。ただし「従三位上」という[[位階]]はない。 ** 正四位下：{{PDFlink|[http://www.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 『国内神名帳』]}}（文政13年（1830年）転写合本、愛知県図書館「貴重和本デジタルライブラリー」より）12コマ。 == 境内 == 本殿はかつて[[八幡造]]であったが、[[明治時代]]に[[神明造]]に建て替えられた上志段味誌、P.115。 また、尾張戸神社の北西側をやや下った場所に小堂がある。これは[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）の参道整備の際に見つかった中世の[[甕棺墓]]を現地に埋め戻して祀ったもので、「甕室明神」と呼ばれている 本文編、P.31本文編、P.44。 == 摂末社 == * 中社（なかやしろ/なかのやしろ、{{ウィキ座標|35|15|18.11|N|137|3|8.99|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|位置|name=境内社：中社}}） *: 祭神：[[菊理媛|菊理媛命]]{{R|神々}}。円墳「中社古墳」（[[#中社古墳|後述]]）の墳丘上に鎮座する。[[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]）に二代藩主・[[徳川光友]]が薬師堂とともに創建『日本の神々』尾張戸神社項。。 * 南社（みなみやしろ / みなみのやしろ、{{ウィキ座標|35|15|12.80|N|137|3|8.66|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|位置|name=境内社：南社}}） *: 祭神：[[イザナギ|伊弉諾命]]{{R|神々}}。円墳「南社古墳」（[[#南社古墳|後述]]）の墳丘上に鎮座する。中社同様、寛文5年（1665年）の創建{{R|神々}}。 File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|中社 File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|南社 == 古墳 == === 尾張戸神社古墳 === {{日本の古墳 |画像 = [[File:Owaribe-jinja honden.JPG|250px]]
墳丘全景（墳頂に尾張戸神社本殿） |名称 = 尾張戸神社古墳 |所属 = [[志段味古墳群]] |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]] |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 墳径27.5[[メートル|m]] |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]前半 |史跡指定 = 国の[[史跡]]「尾張戸神社古墳」（「[[志段味古墳群]]」に包含） |出土品 = 山茶碗片など |}} '''尾張戸神社古墳'''（おわりべじんじゃこふん）は、尾張戸神社の本殿下にある[[円墳]]。[[志段味古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「志段味古墳群」のうち）{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1458|志段味古墳群}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf 「史跡志段味古墳群保存管理計画」]}}（名古屋市ホームページ）。。 [[埴輪]]を伴わない点・[[葺石]]の類似点から、山麓にある[[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥塚古墳]]（志段味古墳群の代表古墳）と同時期の[[4世紀]]前半の築造とされる志段味古墳群をときあかす、P.6。 ==== 規模・構造 ==== この尾張戸神社古墳の形式について、かつては[[前方後方墳]]・[[前方後円墳]]との説があった。[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）あるいはその前年に[[京都大学|京都帝国大学]]の[[梅原末治]]によって概略図が作成されており{{R|honbun31}}、当時は円墳と想定されていた。 [[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）6月に[[名古屋市教育委員会]]文化財保護室によって初めての試掘調査が行われ中日新聞：[http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/aichi/20080621/CK2008062102000041.html 古代の豪族「尾張氏」の墓か　東谷山山頂の古墳３基]、2008年6月21日 {{リンク切れ|date=2013年3月}}、2009年の本調査で[[円墳]]とされた。この調査によって墳丘は直径約27.5[[メートル]]で2段築成と推測されているが、2段目は神社の社殿造営のためにほとんどが削平されている{{R|honbun44}}。 1段目は墳裾から高さ約2メートル、傾斜は30-35度で本文編、P.35、葺石は角礫主体{{R|honbun44}}。基底石の大きさは約30[[センチメートル]]で、上部は10-20センチメートルの礫が使用されており{{R|honbun35}}、[[石英]]の小礫が混ざっていた{{R|honbun44}}。葺石を配置したのちに蒔かれた可能性もあるとされる。1段目のテラス部は幅1.2-1.4メートルで、石英の小礫が葺かれていた{{R|honbun35}}。 2段目は30センチメートルほどの基底石とその上側の一部のみ、残存が確認されている。葺石は1段目と同様に10から20センチメートルの小礫が使用されていた。[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）に本殿裏側が掘り返されて盗掘されかけるも巨石に阻まれた事で被害を免れた記録があるが、この巨石は[[竪穴式石室]]の天井石と考えられている{{R|honbun31}}。周溝は確認されていない{{R|honbun44}}。 [[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）10月6日、古墳域は[[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥塚古墳]]（[[1952年]]に既指定）・中社古墳・南社古墳などとともに「[[志段味古墳群]]」として国の史跡に指定された{{R|志段味古墳群}}。 ==== 出土品 ==== 古墳からの主な出土品は次の通り。 * [[山茶碗]]片 * 四耳壺片（[[古瀬戸]]） * [[土師器]]皿片 * [[瓦]] * 敷瓦 * [[皇宋通宝]] * [[寛永通宝]] * 桐1銭青銅貨（[[大正]]8年・大正9年のもの） 梅原が調査した際の聞き取りで「埴輪が見つかったことがある」との記録があるが、実物は確認されていない。また、近年の調査においても築造時期を明確に示す遺物の出土はない。山茶碗や四耳壺片、土師器皿などは中世遺物で、瓦類は近世のものであった本文編、P.41 - 44。なお、[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）の参道整備の際に見つかった[[甕棺墓]]は15-16世紀の[[常滑焼]]の大甕で、中から針状鉄製品や人骨片、歯牙、木炭片などが見つかっている{{R|honbun31}}。また、かつて墳丘の北東側で[[渥美窯]]で焼かれたと考えられる[[経筒]]容器の破片が見つかった記録があるという{{R|honbun44}}。 === 中社古墳 === [[File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro-kofun.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|中社古墳}}]] '''中社古墳'''（なかやしろこふん）は、尾張戸神社の境内社・中社の社殿下にある[[前方後円墳]]。志段味古墳群を構成する古墳の1つで、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「志段味古墳群」のうち）{{R|国指定|志段味古墳群}}。 東谷山山頂から南約120メートルの地にある、墳丘長約63.5メートルの前方後円墳である境内説明板。。斜面には川原石の葺石があり、円筒埴輪が巡らされている{{R|説明板}}。これらの墳形・埴輪から、尾張戸神社古墳に続く4世紀中頃の築造とされる{{R|説明板}}。 === 南社古墳 === [[File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro-kofun.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|南社古墳}}]] '''南社古墳'''（みなみやしろこふん）は、尾張戸神社の境内社・南社の社殿下にある円墳。志段味古墳群を構成する古墳の1つで、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「志段味古墳群」のうち）{{R|国指定|志段味古墳群}}。 東谷山山頂から南の鞍部を越えた位置にある、直径約30メートルの円墳である{{R|説明板}}。かつては前方後円墳とする説もあった{{R|説明板}}。墳丘の上段には円礫、下段には角礫が葺かれる珍しい形式を有する{{R|説明板}}。出土した円筒埴輪が中社古墳と同形式であることから、本古墳も同じく4世紀中頃の築造とされる{{R|説明板}}。 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === * [[愛知県]][[瀬戸市]]十軒町845 * 愛知県[[名古屋市]][[守山区]]大字上志段味字東谷209 === 交通アクセス === * [[東海旅客鉄道]]（JR東海）[[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]] [[高蔵寺駅]]から、登山口まで徒歩で約15分、山頂まで徒歩で約20分。 * [[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]] [[中水野駅]]から、登山口まで徒歩で約20分、山頂まで徒歩で約15分。 * [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]：高蔵寺行き）で「東谷橋」バス停下車、登山口まで徒歩で約10分、山頂まで徒歩で約20分。 * 東谷山散策路（[[東谷山フルーツパーク]]正門横から）で、山頂まで徒歩で約20分。 === 周辺 === * [[志段味古墳群]] - 上志段味に展開する古墳群。尾張戸神社との関係が指摘される[http://www.museum.city.nagoya.jp/exhibition/special/past/tenji120428.html 企画展「尾張氏☆志段味古墳群をときあかす」]（名古屋市博物館）。。 ** [[白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥塚古墳]] - 志段味古墳群の代表古墳。愛知県内第3位の大きさの前方後円墳。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書、境内説明板（名古屋市教育委員会設置） * 明治神社誌料編纂所編『[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]』（明治神社誌料編纂所、1912年）尾張戸神社項 ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/794}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）794-795コマ参照。 * 『日本歴史地名大系 23 愛知県の地名』（[[平凡社]]、1981年）瀬戸市 尾張戸神社項 * 名古屋市上志段味特定土地区画整理組合 『上志段味誌』、1997年 * [[名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]編 『埋蔵文化財調査報告書62 志段味古墳群（本文編）』名古屋市教育委員会、2011年 * [[名古屋市博物館]] 『尾張氏・志段味古墳群をときあかす』、2012年 * 津田豊彦「尾張戸神社」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海』（[[白水社]]、1987年）） * [[尾張名所図会|『尾張名所図会』 第三巻　尾張戸神社]]、1844年 == 関連項目 == * [[尾張氏]] * [[熱田神宮]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|n|1670604707}} * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html 名古屋市:史跡　尾張戸神社古墳（おわりべじんじゃこふん）] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=尾張戸神社古墳 |1-1=愛知県の古墳 |1-2=守山区の歴史 |1-3=愛知県にある国指定の史跡 |1-4=円墳 |redirect2=中社古墳 |2-1=愛知県の古墳 |2-2=守山区の歴史 |2-3=愛知県にある国指定の史跡 |2-4=前方後円墳 |redirect3=南社古墳 |3-1=愛知県の古墳 |3-2=守山区の歴史 |3-3=愛知県にある国指定の史跡 |3-4=円墳 }} {{デフォルトソート:おわりへしんしや}} [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:守山区の建築物]] [[Category:瀬戸市の歴史]] [[Category:瀬戸市の建築物]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:尾張氏|社おわりへしんしや]]

{{Shrine | Name = Owaribe Shrine | Image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place Names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine Names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ** Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ** Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ** Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ** Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun]

According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place Names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine Names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Ja]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:尾張戸神社]]

These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. {{Shrine | Name = Owaribe Shrine | Image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | Annual festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place Names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine Names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Ja]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place Names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine Names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Ja]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place Names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine Names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Ja]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place Names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine Names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Ja]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Ja]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. [[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]]
