These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-hoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Owari-to Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}} [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}} '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a burial mound known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a burial mound, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:Ngoodbye $1]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a Kofun known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}[[Category:11]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall | location = [[Aichi Prefecture]][[Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[Aichi Prefecture]][[Nagoya]] [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate names = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagoya]]'s [[Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[Seimu]] by [[Miyasuhime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a Kofun known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor [[Shikinaisha]] under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[Meiji period]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] [[Category:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}[[Category:11]] Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Owari-to Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Owari-to Shrine]][[Category:11]] A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine.
