Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Jikken-chō|qid=Q99541272}}; {{ill|Q52306359|qid=Q52306359}} | founded_by = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|zh|天香山命}}; {{ill|Takeinadane|qid=Q11486691}}; {{ill|honoakari|qid=Q10940411}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]'s [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[:en:Shonai River|Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[:en:Seimu|Seimu]] by [[Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[:en:Owari Meisho Zue|Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a Kofun known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[:en:Izanagi-no-Mikoto|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]] [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building |name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Worship hall |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]]{{ill|Jukken-chō (Seto)|ja|十軒町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jukken-chō (Seto)|qq=|1=Jukken-chō (Seto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17172437}} 845
[[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]{{ill|Kamishidami|ja|上志段味|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami|qq=|1=Kamishidami, Nagoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q52306359}} Higashidani 209 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} |Deities = [[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}
{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}} |Sacred Objects = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally 5th year of Emperor Seimu |Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other names = Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin |Festivals = October 15{{Cite book|title=Jinja Meikan|editor=Research Department of Jinja Honchō|publisher=Jinja Honchō|year=1962|page=447}} }} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Mount Togoku, home to Owaribe Shrine]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|qq=|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|qq=|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[sn:Owarito Shrine Kofun]] [[sn:warito Shrine Kofun]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] [[en:Owari-to Shrine]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}

{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}
{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}} |Sacred Objects = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally 5th year of Emperor Seimu |Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other names = Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin |Festivals = October 15{{Cite book|title=Jinja Meikan|editor=Research Department of Jinja Honchō|publisher=Jinja Honchō|year=1962|page=447}} }} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Mount Togoku, home to Owaribe Shrine]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|qq=|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|qq=|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[sn:Owarito Shrine Kofun]] [[sn:warito Shrine Kofun]] [[sn:Owari-to Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11465305]] Wikidata name: Owaribe Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG | location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.256133|N|137.053122|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Jikken-chō|qid=Q99541272}}; {{ill|Q52306359|qid=Q52306359}} | founded_by = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|zh|天香山命}}; {{ill|Takeinadane|qid=Q11486691}}; {{ill|honoakari|qid=Q10940411}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]'s [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[:en:Shonai River|Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[:en:Seimu|Seimu]] by [[Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[:en:Owari Meisho Zue|Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a Kofun known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[:en:Izanagi-no-Mikoto|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building |name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]

It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|qq=|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|qq=|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}

Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Jikken-chō|qid=Q99541272}}; {{ill|Q52306359|qid=Q52306359}} | founded_by = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|zh|天香山命}}; {{ill|Takeinadane|qid=Q11486691}}; {{ill|honoakari|qid=Q10940411}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]'s [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[:en:Shonai River|Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[:en:Seimu|Seimu]] by [[Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[:en:Owari Meisho Zue|Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a Kofun known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[:en:Izanagi-no-Mikoto|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building |name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Worship hall |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]]{{ill|Jukken-chō (Seto)|ja|十軒町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jukken-chō (Seto)|qq=|1=Jukken-chō (Seto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17172437}} 845
[[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]{{ill|Kamishidami|ja|上志段味|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami|qq=|1=Kamishidami, Nagoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q52306359}} Higashidani 209 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} |Deities = [[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}
{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}} |Sacred Objects = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally 5th year of Emperor Seimu |Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other names = Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin |Festivals = October 15{{Cite book|title=Jinja Meikan|editor=Research Department of Jinja Honchō|publisher=Jinja Honchō|year=1962|page=447}} }} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Mount Togoku, home to Owaribe Shrine]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|qq=|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|qq=|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}

{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}
{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}} |Sacred Objects = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally 5th year of Emperor Seimu |Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other names = Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin |Festivals = October 15{{Cite book|title=Jinja Meikan|editor=Research Department of Jinja Honchō|publisher=Jinja Honchō|year=1962|page=447}} }} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Mount Togoku, home to Owaribe Shrine]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|qq=|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|qq=|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Owaribe-jinja]][[ja:尾張戸神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11465305]] Wikidata name: Owaribe Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG | location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]] [[Jūkkenchō (Seto, Aichi)|Jūkkenchō]] 845
[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama-ku]], [[Kamishidami, Nagoya|Ōaza Kamishidami]], Azuma no Tani 209 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.256133|N|137.053122|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]
[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]] | Object of worship = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Jikken-chō|WD=Q99541272}}; {{ill|Q52306359|WD=Q52306359}} | founded_by = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|zh|天香山命}}; {{ill|Takeinadane|WD=Q11486691}}; {{ill|honoakari|WD=Q10940411}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]'s [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[:en:Shonai River|Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[:en:Seimu|Seimu]] by [[Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[:en:Owari Meisho Zue|Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a Kofun known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[:en:Izanagi-no-Mikoto|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building |name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]
Worship hall |location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]]{{ill|Jukken-chō (Seto)|ja|十軒町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jukken-chō (Seto)|qq=|1=Jukken-chō (Seto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17172437}} 845

It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|qq=|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|qq=|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}

Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = (Tradition) 5th year of the reign of Emperor Seimu | Main building = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Oku-no-in of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin | Pilgrimage sites = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]][[Association of Shinto Shrines]] Research Division, "Shrine Directory" (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1962), p. 447. | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Jikken-chō|WD=Q99541272}}; {{ill|Q52306359|WD=Q52306359}} | founded_by = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|zh|天香山命}}; {{ill|Takeinadane|WD=Q11486691}}; {{ill|honoakari|WD=Q10940411}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{Coord list}} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tōgokusan, where Owaribe Shrine is located}}]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社, ''Owaribe Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]'s [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. The shrine is situated atop [[Tōgokusan]], a mountain also known by other names such as "Oku-no-in of Atsuta" and "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Enshrined deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history and explanatory plaque.According to ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' the shrine also enshrines [[Otoyo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]]. These deities are ancestors of the [[Owari clan]]. * '''[[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Ancestral deity of the Owari clan. * '''[[Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Also known as [[Takakuraji-no-Mikoto]]. Eldest son of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. * '''[[Takeinadane-no-Mikoto]]''' *: Twelfth-generation descendant of Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto. According to legend, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto (Takakuraji-no-Mikoto) descended on [[Takakurayama]], across the [[:en:Shonai River|Shonai River]], before relocating to Tōgokusan.Shrine history and explanatory plaque. He is said to have crossed the river riding a white deer, which is commemorated in the name of "Kanou Bridge" (鹿乗橋). == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, recorded in the "Tōgokusan Daimyōjin Sōsō Honki," the shrine was founded in the 5th year of the reign of Emperor [[:en:Seimu|Seimu]] by [[Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto]], a consort of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and sister of Takeinadane-no-Mikoto. The late Edo-period geographical work ''[[:en:Owari Meisho Zue|Owari Meisho Zue]]'' explains that Tōgokusan was originally called "Owariyama" because the Owaribe gods were enshrined there. Over time, "Owari" became "Tōkoku" (a reference to Owari Province), and eventually, the name evolved into "Tōgokusan." The shrine stands atop a Kofun known as "Owaribe Shrine Kofun," part of the [[Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Group]]. These mounds are traditionally attributed to members of the [[Owari clan]]. The shrine's name "Owaribe" is thought to derive from "Owari-be," indicating followers or subjects of the Owari clan. === Later history === The shrine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' (927 CE) as a minor {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} under the name "Owaribe Shrine."''Aichi Prefecture Place names,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. By the late Heian period, it was recorded as "Owaribe Tenjin" in the ''Owari Province Divine names List.'' During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[Tokugawa clan]] became patrons of the shrine, seeing it as an important spiritual site near the [[Kimon]] (northeast direction) of [[Nagoya Castle]]. In 1665, the second lord of [[Owari Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsutomo|Tokugawa Mitsutomo]], ordered the construction of the Chūsha and Minami-sha subordinate shrines, along with a Yakushidō (medicine hall). These structures were rebuilt and maintained under the Tokugawa family's auspices. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in 1872 under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Main building == The main hall of the shrine, originally built in [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] style, was rebuilt in [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].''Kamishidami Historical Records,'' p. 115. A small shrine known as the "Mikuro Myōjin" is located on the northwest slope. This was constructed to enshrine a rediscovered medieval jar burial during pathway construction in 1935.''Shidami Historical Records,'' pp. 31, 44. == Subordinate shrines == * '''Chūsha''' (中社, Central Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[Kukurihime-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Chūsha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. * '''Minami-sha''' (南社, Southern Shrine) ''' Enshrined deity: [[:en:Izanagi-no-Mikoto|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]]. ''' Located on the mound of the Minami-sha Kofun, a Kofun, constructed in 1665.''Nihon no Kamigami,'' entry for Owaribe Shrine. File:Owaribe-jinja Naka-yashiro.JPG|Chūsha File:Owaribe-jinja Minami-yashiro.JPG|Minami-sha == External links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Official Nagoya City page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{infobox religious building |name = Owaribe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Owaribe-jinja haiden-1.JPG|280px]]

[[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]][[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]]{{ill|Kamishidami|ja|上志段味|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamishidami|qq=|1=Kamishidami, Nagoya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q52306359}} Higashidani 209 |coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|22.08|N|137|3|11.24|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Owaribe Shrine}} |Deities = [[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}
{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}} |Sacred Objects = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally 5th year of Emperor Seimu |Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other names = Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin |Festivals = October 15{{Cite book|title=Jinja Meikan|editor=Research Department of Jinja Honchō|publisher=Jinja Honchō|year=1962|page=447}} }} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Mount Togoku, home to Owaribe Shrine]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|qq=|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|qq=|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|qq=|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|qq=|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}

{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}
{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}} |Sacred Objects = |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditionally 5th year of Emperor Seimu |Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Other names = Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta, Tōgoku Myōjin, Tōkoku Myōjin |Festivals = October 15{{Cite book|title=Jinja Meikan|editor=Research Department of Jinja Honchō|publisher=Jinja Honchō|year=1962|page=447}} }} [[File:Owaribe-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] [[File:Togokusan-01.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Mount Togoku, home to Owaribe Shrine]] '''Owaribe Shrine''' (尾張戸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]] of [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]] and [[:en:Seto, Aichi|Seto]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and a former {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated atop {{ill|Mount Togoku|ja|東谷山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Togoku|1=Mount Togoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528351}}, also known as "Inner Sanctuary of Atsuta" or "Tōgoku Myōjin." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:The list of deities follows the shrine's official history.Some sources, such as ''Nihon no Kamigami,'' also include {{ill|Otoyo-no-Mikoto|ja|乎止与命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo-no-Mikoto|1=Otoyo no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97706258}} and {{ill|Amaterasu's daughter|ja|天道日女命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's daughter|1=Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121355874}} as additional enshrined deities. * '''[[:en:Amenohoakari|Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari]]''' ** Ancestor of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]. * '''{{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天香山命}}''' ** Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakuraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高倉下}}, the eldest son of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. * '''{{ill|Tate-i-natane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tate-i-natane|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486691}}''' ** A twelfth-generation descendant of Ame-no-Ho-no-Akari. According to legend, Ame-no-Kaguyama (Takakuraji) crossed the [[:en:Shōnai River|Shōnai River]] on a white deer and moved to Mount Togoku from Mount Takakura, leaving a legacy at the nearby {{ill|Shikanori Bridge|ja|鹿乗橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikanori Bridge|1=Kanori Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676469}}. == History == === Founding === The shrine's tradition, documented in the ''Tōgoku Daimyōjin Sōsōki,'' attributes its founding to the 5th year of Emperor Seimu's reign.Shrine history.{{Cite book|title=Unlocking the Shidami Kofun Cluster|page=74}} It was reportedly established by Princess Miyosuhime, a wife of Prince Yamato Takeru and the sister of Tate-i-natane. In the late Edo and early Meiji periods, the shrine was associated with the local nobility and played a significant role in regional worship.{{Cite book|title=Nagoya City Cultural Properties Survey|year=2008}} == Surrounding Kofun Sites == The shrine is built on the tumulus {{ill|Owaribe Kofun|ja|尾張戸神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owaribe Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾張戸神社|1=Owaribe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465305}}, part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofungun]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000004/4014/hozonkanrikeikaku.pdf Shidami Kofungun Preservation Plan (Nagoya City)] * [https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kyoiku/page/0000004117.html Nagoya City Page on Owaribe Shrine Kofun] {{translated page|ja|尾張戸神社|version=95735526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[ja:尾張戸神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Seto, Aichi]] [[Category:History of Seto]] {{Commons category|Owaribe-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11465305}}
